core_intuitions: The language model IS the policy: text generation is an MDP with partial-sequence states, token actions, a deterministic append-transition, and a single terminal/sparse reward at EOS.

The score-function (REINFORCE) trick turns the gradient of an expected reward into an expectation of R times grad-log-pi, so the reward never needs to be differentiable: sample a response, push up the logprob of every token, scaled by the reward.

A baseline that does not depend on the action removes the meaningless common-mode offset (all-positive rewards push everything up) without adding bias, collapsing variance; the best baseline is the value function, giving the advantage A = Q - V ('was this token better than average?').

GAE is a bias-variance dial (lambda) that interpolates between low-variance/biased one-step TD and high-variance/unbiased Monte Carlo, built as an exponentially-weighted sum of TD residuals computed by one backward recursion; with terminal-only reward it nearly collapses to (R - V) per token.

PPO reuses expensive rollouts for several gradient steps via an importance ratio r_t = pi_theta/pi_old, but that ratio can explode; the clipped surrogate min(...) is a one-sided trust region that removes the incentive to move too far in the rewarding direction without blocking corrective steps.

PPO carries four models (policy, critic, reward, reference) and folds a KL-to-reference leash into the per-token reward; its two-phase rollout/optimize rhythm and the critic are exactly the pain points later methods delete.

existing.name: ppo-mdp-trajectory
existing.improvement: Reads correctly and clearly in both themes: the state/action/reward columns, the deterministic-append vertical axis, the sparse reward (zeros until R(q,o) only at EOS), and the 'The LM IS the policy pi_theta' summary all land. No clipping or overlaps. Foundational and well-executed.

existing.name: ppo-four-model-passes
existing.issue: Dark theme: the 'tokens o1..o_T' label sits on an opaque near-white highlight box that clips against the dark background and looks like a rendering glitch; the top-right 'sample o=o1..o_T' caption collides with the policy->response arrow; and the three lower model cards render as flat white boxes in dark mode rather than a theme-aware surface tint.
existing.improvement: Make the 'tokens o1..o_T' label inherit the page background (no opaque white box) in both themes. Nudge the 'sample o=o1..o_T' caption up/left so it clears the policy->response arrow. Give the reward/value/reference cards a subtle theme-aware surface fill (light-tinted in light mode, dark-tinted in dark mode) instead of a hardcoded near-white, keeping the accent border on the two trained models (policy, critic) and dashed neutral border on the two frozen ones. Content and layout are otherwise strong; keep the 'four passes' legend and the advantage box.

existing.name: ppo-clip
existing.issue: Two problems. (1) Redundancy: this two-panel L_CLIP-vs-r plot (A>0 / A<0) duplicates ppo-clip-surrogate-curves ~30 lines later, which covers the same clip curves more cleanly; two near-identical clip plots is decoration, not added intuition. (2) Quality: the bottom min() equation renders with overlapping text ('r.A' collides with 'clip'), the minus signs in '1-eps' render as box glyphs, and the right-panel top labels/'flat gradient=0/no extra reward' text crowd and overlap the axis. Meanwhile the marker sits at the very top of 'The PPO objective' section, right before 'From policy gradient to a surrogate we can reuse' -- a slot whose real conceptual gap (why we reuse rollouts, why the ratio is dangerous) has NO figure.
existing.improvement: Replace with an importance-sampling motivation figure that fills the actual gap at this section opener and eliminates the clip-curve redundancy. Show the reuse loop: 'generate rollouts ONCE (expensive autoregressive decode)' -> 'take SEVERAL gradient steps on the same batch'. After step 1 the data is off-policy, so each token is reweighted by the ratio r_t = pi_theta(o_t|s_t)/pi_old(o_t|s_t). Illustrate the danger with a small strip of tokens: most have r_t near 1.0 (safe), but one rare-under-old token has r_t blowing up (e.g. small illustrative integers like a jump from ratio 1 to 8), and r_t times a large advantage takes a catastrophic step that can destroy the policy in one update. End with the punchline arrow: 'this is why PPO must BOUND the ratio' -> pointing forward to the clip. Use theme-safe accents (a neutral 'safe' zone around r=1 and a warning-accent 'blow-up' zone), small illustrative integers only, no invented benchmark numbers. This leaves ppo-clip-surrogate-curves as the single, clean clip-curve figure.

existing.name: ppo-clip-surrogate-curves
existing.improvement: Clean and correct in both themes: the two panels (A>0 boost up to 1+e then flat; A<0 suppress down to 1-e then flat), the shaded clipped zones, the 'flat -- gradient 0' annotation, and the epsilon footnote all read clearly with no overlaps. This is the well-executed clip-curve figure; it makes retiring the redundant ppo-clip safe.

existing.name: ppo-iteration-two-phase-loop
existing.improvement: Excellent and comprehensive in both themes: the numbered rollout phase (torch.no_grad, runs once) vs optimization phase (PPO_EPOCHS over shuffled minibatches), the frozen experience buffer, the early-stop-on-KL node, and the 'theta_old <- theta' hand-off all land. Minor optional polish: the bottom 'theta_old <- theta ...' caption sits very close to the frame's bottom edge in dark mode -- give it a touch more bottom padding so it never risks clipping. Otherwise keep as-is.

new_figures.name: reinforce-baseline-variance
new_figures.concept: Why a baseline is a free-lunch variance killer: all-positive rewards push every token up (incoherent); subtracting the mean leaves only the discriminative advantage.
new_figures.rationale: The common-mode-offset intuition is the conceptual seed of BOTH the PPO critic and the GRPO group mean, and the chapter devotes an entire worked example and a whole section to it, yet no figure serves it. Seeing 'every arrow points up, which is impossible since probabilities sum to 1' next to 'subtract the mean -> some up, some down, offset gone' makes the single most reused idea in policy-gradient RL click instantly.
new_figures.placement: Section 'Baselines, the value function, and the advantage' -> subsection 'The baseline trick' (anchor after the one-line baseline-is-unbiased proof), or equivalently right after the 'Worked example: variance of REINFORCE with vs. without a baseline'.
new_figures.brief: A two-panel before/after infographic driven by the chapter's own toy bandit (three candidate responses A, B, C; reward-model scores R = 10.0, 9.5, 9.0; mean R-bar = 9.65). LEFT panel titled 'REINFORCE, no baseline'. Draw three horizontal response rows (A, B, C), each labeled with its reward as a tall bar rising from a zero line to ~9-10 -- all three bars nearly the same height, all large and positive. From each response draw a fat UP arrow on its tokens (all three arrows point up, same direction). Overlay a bracket spanning the shared bar height labeled 'common-mode offset ~9.65 (meaningless)' and a tiny bracket on the small height differences labeled 'useful signal (the spread, ~5% of magnitude)'. Add a callout: 'every response pushed UP -- incoherent, since sum of pi = 1; the real signal drowns in the offset -> huge variance'. RIGHT panel titled 'With a baseline b = mean reward'. Same three rows, but now bars are the ADVANTAGES R_i - R-bar: A = +0.35 (short bar above the zero line, UP arrow), B = -0.15 (short bar below, DOWN arrow), C = -0.65 (longer bar below, DOWN arrow). The zero line is emphasized as 'baseline'. Callout: 'offset collapses to zero; only the spread survives -> A up, B and C down; variance drops by ~ (mean/spread)^2'. Bottom strip, one line: 'A baseline that does not depend on the action changes variance, not the mean (unbiased). The variance-minimizing baseline is the value function V(s_t) -> the advantage A = Q - V.' Use a positive accent for the up/above-baseline and a warning/negative accent for down/below-baseline, theme-safe (must work in light and dark; no hardcoded white/black fills). Small integers only; these numbers come straight from the chapter's worked example, invent nothing new. Everything inside the viewBox, clear left-to-right reading order, no overlapping labels.

new_figures.name: gae-bias-variance-dial
new_figures.concept: GAE as a bias-variance dial (lambda) between one-step TD and Monte Carlo, built from an exponentially-weighted sum of TD residuals via one backward recursion; with terminal-only reward it nearly collapses to (R - V) per token.
new_figures.rationale: GAE is a full section with a section header literally named 'The bias-variance dial', a backward-recursion code block, and a hand-traced worked example -- but it has zero figures. The lambda interpolation, the (gamma*lambda)^l decaying weights over the delta sequence, and the LLM-specific collapse under sparse terminal reward are exactly the kind of mechanism a single well-designed static frame makes intuitive, saving the reader from reconstructing it from the recursion by hand.
new_figures.placement: Section 'Generalized Advantage Estimation (GAE)' -> subsection 'The bias-variance dial' (anchor right after the GAE(gamma,lambda) exponential-weighting equation, before the code block).
new_figures.brief: A single static figure in two stacked bands. TOP BAND -- 'the dial'. Draw a horizontal slider/dial from left endpoint 'lambda = 0' to right endpoint 'lambda = 1', with a marker sitting near the right at 'lambda ~ 0.95 (typical for LLMs)'. Label the left end 'one-step TD: A_t = delta_t -- LOW variance, BIASED when V is wrong' and the right end 'Monte Carlo: A_t = (full return) - V(s_t) -- UNBIASED, HIGH variance'. Under the track, two opposing gradient wedges: a 'bias' wedge shrinking left-to-right and a 'variance' wedge growing left-to-right, so the reader sees the trade being dialed. MIDDLE/BOTTOM BAND -- 'how GAE is built'. Show a short token timeline t, t+1, t+2, t+3 along the response, and above each a box for its TD residual delta_t = r_t + gamma*V(s_{t+1}) - V(s_t) (annotate delta as 'reward you got + critic value of where you landed - critic value of where you were'). Draw the GAE sum A_t = sum_l (gamma*lambda)^l * delta_{t+l} as a row of decaying weight bars over delta_t, delta_{t+1}, delta_{t+2}, ... : weights 1, gamma*lambda, (gamma*lambda)^2, ... shown as bars shrinking to the right (small illustrative decay, e.g. 1.00, 0.95, 0.90, symbolic is fine). Add a curved backward-pointing arrow labeled 'one backward recursion: A_t = delta_t + gamma*lambda * A_{t+1}' to convey it is accumulated from the last token leftward. INSET / footnote box (LLM-specific payoff): 'Reward is terminal-only, so interior delta_t = gamma*V(s_{t+1}) - V(s_t) is pure critic bootstrapping; as lambda -> 1 GAE collapses to (terminal R) - V(s_t) shared across tokens -- which is why critic-free methods (GRPO/RLOO) lose so little.' Theme-safe accents only, no hardcoded primary colors, small illustrative integers/decimals, invent no benchmark numbers. Keep the dial legible, the delta timeline uncrowded, and the whole thing within the viewBox with a clear top-to-bottom reading order.

summary: This is a long, concept-dense chapter that is already fairly well-figured (5 existing), but with one redundancy and two genuine gaps. AUDIT: ppo-mdp-trajectory (keep -- clean, correct, both themes), ppo-clip-surrogate-curves (keep -- the clean clip-curve figure), ppo-iteration-two-phase-loop (keep -- excellent, tiny bottom-padding nit), ppo-four-model-passes (refine -- dark-mode white-box label glitch on 'tokens o1..o_T', a top-right caption overlapping the response arrow, and non-theme-aware white model cards), ppo-clip (REPLACE -- it duplicates ppo-clip-surrogate-curves AND has a garbled overlapping min() equation with broken minus glyphs; repurpose its well-placed slot at the top of 'The PPO objective' into the missing importance-sampling motivation, i.e. reuse-rollouts -> ratio r_t -> ratio can explode -> must bound). NEW: (1) reinforce-baseline-variance -- the common-mode-offset intuition (the seed of both the critic and the GRPO group mean) that a whole section and worked example develop but no figure serves; (2) gae-bias-variance-dial -- the lambda dial, the decaying-weight sum of TD residuals, the backward recursion, and the terminal-reward collapse, for a full section that currently has zero figures. Both new figures are static: a single frame conveys each mechanism, so no animation is warranted.
